_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: TX RGV House delegation HD34-38: evidence-finalize denise-villalobos, oscar-longoria, sergio-munoz, janie-lopez, erin-gamez. HARD anchors = House Journal roll calls (journals.house.texas.gov), Texas Freedom Index itemized votes, TAL/TRTL/TVA/TFR scorecards. Party-line marquee votes with NAMED crossovers (SB2 85-63 all-R yeas w/ Phelan/VanDeaver no; SB14 87-56 w/ 4 named Dem yeas) treated HARD on the recorded side by exclusion. Documented Valley-Dem crossovers captured: Longoria YEA SB10 Ten Commandments; Munoz joint-author HB7 border + YES HB1056 money + NO HB1738 (kept sodomy statute); these are scored TRUE. Absences (Longoria SB2/SB17/HB1927; unconfirmed individual culture-war roll calls) left NULL, never inferred. Gamez = progressive LGBTQ-caucus Dem, TAL 52% is consensus-emergency-bill noise, not pro-life conviction.

denise-villalobos@TX.notes_set: TX HD-34 (Nueces/Corpus Christi), Republican freshman; sworn Jan 14 2025 (89th Leg) after flipping the open seat from retiring Dem Abel Herrero; running for re-election 2026 (HD-34 is a flagged battleground). Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025); Texas Values Action Faith & Family 87% (2025); TFR Index 60 (middling). PRIMARY AUTHOR of HB2306 (removes parole eligibility for traffickers of child/disabled victims), signed June 20 2025. Voted YES SB2 (2025 universal ESA voucher; party-bloc, only GOP no-votes Phelan/VanDeaver). Endorsed by Corpus Christi Police Officers Assn. No cross-party deviation found; light culture-war messaging ('people over politics'). Individual marquee roll calls (capitol/LegiScan) were not machine-retrievable this pass — scored from the issue-group scorecards (HARD aggregate) + the confirmed SB2 bloc vote. Researched 2026-06-19.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 100% pro-life (89th, 2025) across SB31/SB33/HB7 abortion-restriction bills.

1.note: No abolition vote/advocacy; 100% rating reflects restrictions, not abolition.

4.note: No abortion-PAC funding verified either way; absence of evidence not credited.

0.note: No documented man-woman marriage statement.

2.note: Texas Values Action Faith & Family 87% (2025), whose 89th slate centers HB229 ('What is a Woman'/biological-sex definition) — affirms biological sex.

4.note: No specific LGBTQ-promotion vote individually confirmed.

0.note: Voted YES SB2 (2025 universal ESA/school-choice); all 85 yeas were Republicans, only Phelan/VanDeaver defected — she is on the yes side.

1.note: TVA 87% slate includes SB12 (Parents' Bill of Rights) — parental consent on minor health/social-transition.

2.note: No K-12 CRT/SOGI vote individually confirmed beyond the aggregate; left null.

0.note: No documented public-profession vote.

3.note: TVA 87% slate includes SB10 (Ten Commandments) and SB11 (school prayer/Bible reading) — public-square Christian symbols/prayer.

0.note: No documented CBDC/legal-tender position.

2.note: TFR Index 60 (middling, not a Taxpayer Champion); no balanced-budget-amendment vote — left null.

0.note: No documented paper-ballot position.
Endorsed by Corpus Christi Police Officers Assn; campaign affirmatively backs local police — accepted law-enforcement endorsement.

2.note: Primary author of HB2306 (2025, signed) removing parole eligibility for traffickers of child/disabled victims — proportional punishment of evildoers.

0.note: No documented 10A/nullification bill.
No documented barrier/military vote.

4.note: Individual SB17 farmland vote not confirmed.

0.note: Constitutional carry predates her term; no specific vote/statement.

1.note: Pro-gun-privacy comment only; no specific red-flag/AWB/registry vote.

oscar-longoria@TX.notes_set: TX HD-35 (Hidalgo/Cameron), Democrat; in office since Jan 2013 (7th term), running for re-election 2026 (won 2024 unopposed). Mixed Valley Democrat. House-Journal-verified roll calls: NAY SB8 (2021 Heartbeat), NAY SB14 (2023 minor gender-transition ban; not among the 3 Dem crossovers), NAY SB12 (2025 anti-SOGI parental rights), NAY SB8 (2025 county/ICE cooperation). DOCUMENTED CONSERVATIVE CROSSOVER: YEA SB10 (2025 Ten Commandments, RV#3503, journal-confirmed in Yeas). Also notable: 65% pro-life rating from BOTH Texas Right to Life and Texas Alliance for Life (89th) — high for a Democrat, driven by coauthorships, though he voted against the 2021 Heartbeat floor bill. CORRECTED stale web claims: he was ABSENT (not a No) on SB2 vouchers, SB17 farmland, and HB1927 constitutional carry — those left null, not contrary. Researched 2026-06-19.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB8 (2021 Heartbeat Act, RV#784, listed in Nays) — a documented vote against protecting life from early gestation. (Mitigating: 65% TAL/TRTL rating from coauthorships.)

1.note: No abolition vote either direction; SB8 was a restriction.

4.note: No donor record located either way.

0.note: No documented man-woman marriage statement.

2.note: Voted NAY on SB14 (2023 minor gender-transition ban, RV#1688); NOT among the 3 Dem crossovers — recorded against affirming biological sex.

4.note: Voted NAY on SB12 (2025 parental rights / bans K-12 SOGI instruction & social-transitioning, RV#3506) — against the opposes-LGBTQ-promotion position.

0.note: ABSENT on SB2 (2025 voucher) third reading and the prior day — no recorded position; corrected stale 'voted No' web claim.

1.note: Voted NAY on SB14 (2023), which bars gender interventions for minors — against parental-consent/anti-intervention position.

2.note: Voted NAY on SB12 (2025) banning K-12 SOGI/DEI instruction.

0.note: No documented public-profession vote.

3.note: CROSSOVER: voted YEA on SB10 (2025 Ten Commandments in classrooms, RV#3503, listed in Yeas 82-46) — a Democrat backing public-square Christian display.

0.note: No documented CBDC/legal-tender position.
No documented paper-ballot position.

1.note: SB1 (2021) individual roll-call line not pinned to him; left null rather than inferred.

0.note: No documented affirmative pro-police action (criminal-defense attorney by profession).
No documented 10A/nullification bill.
No documented barrier/military vote (border-security interim committee = assignment, not a vote).

2.note: Voted NAY on SB8 (2025, RV#3502) mandating county/sheriff ICE-cooperation agreements — did NOT cross over on enforcement.

4.note: ABSENT on SB17 (2025 foreign-land ban) — no recorded position.

0.note: ABSENT-excused on HB1927 (2021 constitutional carry) — NOT a No; corrected stale web claim.

sergio-munoz@TX.notes_set: TX HD-36 (Hidalgo/McAllen-Mission-Pharr), Democrat; in office since Jan 2011, attorney; running for re-election 2026 (GOP primary canceled). Mixed Valley Democrat with THREE documented conservative crossovers: (1) JOINT AUTHOR of HB7 (2023 border-security/Texas Border Force barrier funding); (2) YES on HB1056 (2025 gold/silver constitutional money); (3) NO on HB1738 (2025) — voted to KEEP the homosexual-conduct statute (Freedom Index scored conservative). Otherwise caucus-line: 'Pro-Choice' rating with NO votes on 2021 abortion restrictions (HB1280/SB8/SB4); NO on SB2 (2025 voucher, party-line); NO on HB1927 (2021 constitutional carry, not among the 7 named Dem crossovers). Texas Alliance for Life 61% (moderate-high for a Dem); Freedom Index lifetime 17%. HB796 Sovereignty Act YES was scored NON-conservative by Freedom Index (weakened version) — left null. Researched 2026-06-19.

0.note: Voted NO on HB1280 (2021 trigger ban), SB8 (Heartbeat), SB4 (medication-abortion ban); rated 'Pro-Choice' — against personhood-from-conception in law.

4.note: No donor record located (TAL 61% suggests no aggressive abortion-industry alignment, but not scored).

0.note: No documented man-woman marriage statement.

2.note: SB14 (2023 minor gender-care ban) passed 87-56 with only 4 named Dem crossovers; Munoz not among them — recorded against affirming biological sex by exclusion.

4.note: CROSSOVER: voted NO on HB1738 (2025) repealing the homosexual-conduct statute — i.e., to retain it; Freedom Index scored conservative.

0.note: SB2 (2025 universal ESA voucher) passed 86-61 with every present Democrat opposed and no Valley-Dem crossover — recorded against school choice.

1.note: SB14 NO (by named-crossover exclusion) — against parental-consent/anti-gender-intervention position.

0.note: Catholic affiliation only; not a policy position on this cell.

3.note: SB10 (2025) individual roll-call not isolated; Dems split on it, so no bloc inference — left null.

0.note: CROSSOVER: voted YES on HB1056 (2025) recognizing gold/silver as constitutional legal tender — sound-money / anti-CBDC direction.
No documented paper-ballot position.

1.note: SB1 (2021) individual roll-call not isolated; left null rather than inferred.

0.note: No documented affirmative pro-police action (criminal-defense attorney).
Voted YES on HB796 (Sovereignty Act) but Freedom Index scored that YES non-conservative (weakened version); NO on HJR98 Article V — net does not align, left null.

3.note: No clean pro-sovereignty position (HB796 vote scored non-conservative).

0.note: CROSSOVER: JOINT AUTHOR of HB7 (2023) funding border-barrier infrastructure and the Texas Border Force.

4.note: SB17 (2025 farmland) individual vote not isolated.

0.note: HB1927 (2021 permitless carry) passed 84-56; only 7 named Democrats crossed over and Munoz was NOT among them — recorded against constitutional carry by exclusion.

janie-lopez@TX.notes_set: TX HD-37 (Willacy + part of Cameron, San Benito/Brownsville), Republican; sworn Jan 2023 (first elected 2022, flipped a Dem seat); WON the March 3 2026 GOP primary 69-31 over Kristin Luckey, advancing to the Nov general. Endorsed by Trump, Abbott, Cruz, Texas Right to Life PAC. 100% on BOTH Texas Alliance for Life and Texas Right to Life (89th). Freedom-Index-itemized YES votes: SB2 (school choice), HB1056 (gold/silver money), HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act), SB1070 (ERIC voter-data exit), HB2837 (bars firearm merchant-code surveillance/registries); NO on HB1738 (homosexual-conduct repeal = conservative). A swing-district moderate by Freedom Index (50-60%) / YCT 65 — the moderation is on process/spending votes (Calendars + Appropriations, backs large education funding), NOT on the rubric cells, where her documented record is uniformly conservative. SB14/HB229 (biological sex) passed with the R bloc; no R defection reported. Researched 2026-06-19.

0.note: 100% on BOTH Texas Alliance for Life and Texas Right to Life (89th, 2025) — documented pro-life voting record.

1.note: No abolition vote/advocacy; ratings reflect incremental restrictions.

4.note: TRTL PAC endorsement; no abortion-PAC money, but absence not credited.

0.note: No documented man-woman marriage statement.

2.note: SB14 (2023 minor gender-care ban) passed 87-56 on the R bloc with no R defection reported; Lopez (R) on the yes side — affirms biological sex.

4.note: Voted NO on HB1738 (2025) repealing the homosexual-conduct statute — Freedom Index conservative position against LGBTQ legal normalization.

0.note: Voted YES on SB2 (2025 statewide ESA/voucher) and against the budget amendment to bar voucher funds.

1.note: SB14 YES (R bloc) bars gender interventions for minors — parental-consent/anti-intervention alignment.

2.note: Platform opposes 'woke DEI' but no individual K-12 SOGI roll-call confirmed; left null.

0.note: Identifies with Christian values (bio), but no policy vote on this cell.

3.note: SB10/SB11 individual roll-call not confirmed; flagged-moderate, no bloc inference — left null.

0.note: Voted YES on HB1056 (2025) recognizing gold/silver as legal tender — sound-money / anti-CBDC direction.

2.note: Appropriations member who backs large spending bills (Freedom Index 50-60%); no balanced-budget-amendment vote — left null.

0.note: No documented paper-ballot position.

4.note: ERIC-exit scored under refuse_federal_overreach; no Zuckerbucks/foreign-interference vote here.

0.note: No documented affirmative pro-police action.
Voted YES on HB796 (2025 Texas Sovereignty Act) — federal-act nullification/anti-commandeering.

4.note: Voted YES on SB1070 (2023) withdrawing Texas from the ERIC voter-data compact — opposing external capture of state election data.

erin-gamez@TX.notes_set: TX HD-38 (Cameron/Brownsville), Democrat; sworn March 2022 (special election), re-elected 2024 unopposed; running 2026. Deputy Whip of the House Democratic Caucus and a member of the Texas House LGBTQ Caucus; broke quorum Aug 2025 over GOP redistricting. Down-the-line progressive with no conservative crossover found on the rubric cells. House-confirmed contrary votes: NO on SB14 (2023 minor gender-care ban; not among the 4 named Dem crossovers) and NO on SB2 (2025 voucher, 85-63 all-R yeas). Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career F (30/100, 2025). NOTE: her Texas Alliance for Life 52% (89th) is NOT pro-life conviction — it reflects votes on near-unanimous medical-emergency bills (SB31 Life of the Mother Act, 129-6), so life cells left null rather than scored either way. No documented anti-life floor vote (took office after SB8 2021). Researched 2026-06-19.

0.note: No documented affirmation; TAL 52% reflects consensus emergency bills (SB31), not personhood — left null, not inferred from party.

1.note: No abolition vote; no documented anti-life floor vote (took office after SB8 2021).
